title: helianthemum ocymoides subsp. elongatum
intro: Helianthemum ocymoides subsp. elongatum, also known as rockrose, is a plant species belonging to the Cistaceae family. It is a low-growing, spreading shrub native to the Mediterranean region, valued for its medicinal and ornamental properties. The plant contains bioactive compounds such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, contributing to its medicinal value. Rockrose is used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, to alleviate respiratory issues, soothe digestive problems, and promote wound healing, among other health benefits.
